hypothesis: After routed, gated, discrete, and low-rank variants all failed badly at `K=24`, the next severe-stagnation move should test a fundamentally different structured sparsity family without trunks, gates, or codebooks. `bucketed_topk` at `K=24, EF=12` may improve the mid-K tradeoff by partitioning activations into simpler local competition pools before selection, potentially reducing redundant support allocation versus plain global top-k.
summary: Run a pure architecture comparison at `K=24, EF=12` with `bucketed_topk` under the pinned recipe `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`. This stays within severe-stagnation policy by making another architecture-only jump instead of any scalar tweak or repair round, while keeping evaluation anchored on the frontier-relevant mid-K slot.

I also avoided code edits because recent repair attempts were policy-rejected; this keeps the round cleanly architecture-only with all recipe fields pinned.
notes_to_memory: Round 55 continues severe-stagnation recovery with a pure `bucketed_topk` architecture test after `routed` also failed badly at K=24.
